---
title: "Guide Overview"
---

This guide takes you from your first table to advanced styling, interactivity, and Shiny integration. Read in order, or jump to a chapter.

## Reading order

| # | Chapter | What you'll learn |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | [Quick Start](quick-start.qmd) | Install tabviz and render your first table |
| 2 | [Columns](columns.qmd) | The `col_*()` / `viz_*()` system and column groups |
| 3 | [Row Styling](row-styling.qmd) | Bold, italic, color, indentation, badges |
| 4 | [Cell Styling](cell-styling.qmd) | Conditional formatting per cell |
| 5 | [Row Groups](row-groups.qmd) | Collapsible hierarchical sections |
| 6 | [Forest Plots](visualizations/forest-plots.qmd) | `viz_forest()` and multi-effect rows |
| 7 | [Themes](themes.qmd) | Preset themes and `set_*()` modifiers |
| 8 | [Fluent API](fluent-api.qmd) | Pipe-based post-build modifiers |
| 9 | [Interactivity](interactivity.qmd) | Sort, filter, drag, edit, tooltips |
| 10 | [Split Plots](split-plots.qmd) | Sidebar navigation for subgroups |
| 11 | [Shiny Integration](shiny.qmd) | Reactive tables and proxy updates |
| 12 | [Export](export.qmd) | Save to SVG, PNG, or PDF |

## Two entry points

`tabviz()` is the general-purpose constructor. `forest_plot()` is a thin convenience wrapper for the common case where you only need point + lower + upper.

```r
# Full control
tabviz(data,
  label = "study",
  columns = list(
    col_text("category"),
    col_numeric("value"),
    viz_forest(point = "hr", lower = "lo", upper = "hi"),
    col_sparkline("trend")
  )
)

# Quick forest plot
forest_plot(data,
  point = "hr", lower = "lower", upper = "upper",
  label = "study",
  scale = "log", null_value = 1
)
```

:::{.callout-tip}
## When to reach for `forest_plot()`
Use `forest_plot()` when the table is "labels + a forest plot + maybe a CI string." The moment you need extra columns, badges, or column groups, switch to `tabviz()`.
:::

## Where else to look

- [Cheatsheet](../cheatsheet.qmd) — one-page lookup for functions and arguments.
- [Concepts](../concepts/index.qmd) — architecture, mental models, and the styling cascade.
- [Reference](../reference/index.qmd) — full API documentation.
- [Gallery](../gallery/index.qmd) — runnable examples by domain.
- [Troubleshooting](../reference/troubleshooting.qmd) — common issues and fixes.
